claim_boundary: Pinned public-source seeds mapped into Invart local demos; original incidents are not replayed.

sources.title: ClawHub exposes a public skill and plugin registry surface
sources.observed_risk: Public agent skill registries create a supply-chain surface where SKILL.md instructions, supporting files, plugins, moderation state, and install flows need pre-runtime inspection.
sources.short_excerpt: the public skill registry for OpenClaw
sources.before_signal: Scan SKILL.md, supporting files, package metadata, publisher identity, version, and moderation status before the agent run.
sources.during_signal: Record skill-originated tool calls, capability grant use, file reads, shell commands, and network egress.
sources.after_signal: Audit explains which skill or plugin source contributed to a decision, approval, deny, or weak coverage finding.
sources.claim_boundary: Public registry seed only; original third-party packages are not replayed.

sources.title: ClawHub moderation states imply install-time trust boundaries
sources.observed_risk: Moderation holds, hidden listings, quarantine, and revoked listings are supply-chain signals that should be captured before an agent can use a skill or plugin.
sources.short_excerpt: Moderation holds are meant to protect users
sources.before_signal: Import registry moderation state and map held, hidden, quarantined, or revoked listings to profile-controlled risk levels.
sources.during_signal: If the agent attempts to use a held or hidden listing, require approval or deny according to enterprise policy.
sources.after_signal: Proof shows whether the listing state was observed, mediated, and enforced before use.
sources.claim_boundary: Registry moderation seed only; Invart does not claim the original listing was installed or replayed.

sources.title: SkillSieve reports large-scale empirical risk in ClawHub skills
sources.observed_risk: Large registries of natural-language agent skills can hide prompt injection, social engineering, and obfuscated payloads that simple regex scanners miss.
sources.before_signal: Use layered scanning: metadata, regex, semantic review, and profile-specific escalation for suspicious skill content.
sources.during_signal: Tie skill-originated actions to the scanned skill identity and capability grant.
sources.after_signal: Report which layer produced the risk signal and whether LLM review upgraded but did not downgrade deterministic critical findings.

mapped_trajectory.question: did the scan preserve recall without overclaiming enforcement?

sources.claim_boundary: Research seed only; the paper corpus is not bundled or replayed by the default demo.

sources.title: Malicious npm packages can backdoor Claude Code sessions through hooks
sources.observed_risk: Package installs can place Claude Code hook files that execute on future sessions, surviving beyond the initial package installation moment.
sources.short_excerpt: SessionStart fires every time the project opens
sources.before_signal: Scan node_modules, lockfiles, .claude settings, hook binaries, hashes, and unexpected SessionStart entries.
sources.during_signal: Normalize hook-originated events into mediation records and block or pause high-risk shell/network behavior.
sources.after_signal: Audit reconstructs package install source, hook persistence, session start trigger, and outcome.
sources.claim_boundary: Public report seed only; Invart default demo does not install or replay the malicious packages.

sources.title: Malware-Slop npm package targeted Claude AI workspace files
sources.observed_risk: AI workspaces and developer directories become exfiltration targets when package scripts can recursively read files and upload them through GitHub APIs.
sources.short_excerpt: AI workspaces are becoming part of the developer attack surface
sources.before_signal: Flag install scripts, suspicious package provenance, GitHub token access, and reads under AI workspace directories.
sources.during_signal: Taint workspace file reads and connect them to GitHub API upload attempts.
sources.after_signal: Path graph answers file read to token use to external upload.

mapped_trajectory.question: what data path reached external upload?

sources.claim_boundary: Public report seed only; local demo uses an equivalent trajectory and does not replay the original package.

sources.title: Claude Code issue reports destructive recursive deletion and incomplete logging
sources.observed_risk: Destructive shell commands can cause large local data loss, and incomplete command logging can prevent forensic reconstruction.
sources.short_excerpt: deleted all user files in my home directory
sources.before_signal: Profile requires destructive shell mediation and project-root containment.
sources.during_signal: Record exact command, matched rule, approval requirement, shim decision, and side-effect outcome.
sources.after_signal: Audit shows the command, policy reason, coverage state, and whether enforcement happened before execution.
sources.claim_boundary: Public issue seed only; the local demo blocks an equivalent destructive command instead of replaying user data loss.

sources.title: User report describes Claude Code leaking a secret during a local session
sources.observed_risk: Agent shell or network access combined with environment secrets can create outbound leakage paths that users notice only after egress.
sources.short_excerpt: Claude Code leaked one of my secrets
sources.before_signal: Record env key inventory and redact values before the run.
sources.during_signal: Taint the session after secret read or secret-shaped content, then pause outbound network or git push-like egress.
sources.after_signal: Proof answers which credential keys were in boundary and which action attempted egress.
sources.claim_boundary: Public user-report seed only; the local demo models the risk class and does not replay the original session.

sources.title: Public report describes an AI agent deleting a production database
sources.observed_risk: Broad cloud or database credentials can turn a wrong agent action into destructive production infrastructure mutation.
sources.short_excerpt: deleted the company's entire production database and backups
sources.before_signal: Record cloud credential boundary and profile-required approval for production mutation.
sources.during_signal: Mediate cloud provider commands or API calls that mutate database, backup, CI, or deploy resources.
sources.after_signal: Audit shows principal, credential boundary, policy, approval state, outcome, and coverage.
sources.claim_boundary: Public report seed only; Invart does not replay or access any production infrastructure.

sources.title: MCP puppeteer server issue reports SSRF and indirect prompt injection
sources.observed_risk: Browser/tool servers can bridge untrusted page content to agent tool calls, enabling SSRF, indirect prompt injection, or sandbox bypass behavior.
sources.short_excerpt: SSRF, Indirect Prompt Injection, and sandbox bypass
sources.before_signal: Inventory MCP server permissions, browser access, network reachability, and sandbox boundaries.
sources.during_signal: Broker tool calls, fold raw page content, taint untrusted tool results, and pause internal-network egress.
sources.after_signal: Coverage report shows whether MCP calls were transparent, mediated, or enforced.
sources.claim_boundary: Public issue seed only; local demo models the MCP risk class and does not replay or exploit the original server.

sources.title: OWASP MCP guidance describes tool poisoning and indirect prompt injection
sources.observed_risk: MCP tool metadata and tool results can carry instructions that redirect agent behavior across a trust boundary.
sources.short_excerpt: indirect prompt injection attack targeting AI agents
sources.before_signal: Scan tool descriptions, scopes, and server provenance before exposing tools to the agent.
sources.during_signal: Treat tool output as untrusted input and prevent it from authorizing destructive or credential-bearing actions.
sources.after_signal: Audit explains whether a tool result influenced a later action and which policy blocked or allowed it.

mapped_trajectory.question: did tool output influence the shell command?

sources.claim_boundary: Taxonomy seed only; local demo creates an equivalent tool-result trajectory and does not replay a live attack.

sources.title: Gemini CLI vulnerability report describes silent command execution risk
sources.observed_risk: Agent CLIs that execute commands need independent mediation, because prompt injection or sandbox bypass can trigger command execution without clear user approval.
sources.short_excerpt: execute it without warning or ask for approval
sources.before_signal: Detect Gemini CLI config and sandbox mode, then mark direct unmanaged execution as a coverage gap.
sources.during_signal: Route command execution through managed launcher or wrapper mediation.
sources.after_signal: Audit distinguishes vendor sandbox state, Invart mediation, and actual enforcement coverage.

mapped_trajectory.question: was command execution vendor-owned or Invart-mediated?

sources.claim_boundary: Public report seed only; local demo models command mediation and does not replay or exploit Gemini CLI.

sources.title: OpenAI Codex documentation distinguishes approval modes and sandboxing
sources.observed_risk: Agent approval modes and sandbox profiles are vendor-owned control states that should be inventoried but not confused with Invart enforcement.
sources.short_excerpt: Approval modes define when Codex asks for permission
sources.before_signal: Inventory configured approval mode, sandbox mode, network policy, and whether startup goes through Invart.
sources.during_signal: If direct Codex execution bypasses Invart, mark vendor-owned or unmanaged coverage rather than claiming mediation.
sources.after_signal: RC gate reports coverage gaps until a managed launcher, wrapper, or stronger hook path is active.

mapped_trajectory.question: is the session local-ready or external-pending?

sources.claim_boundary: Official documentation seed only; it describes vendor control modes and is not replayed as an incident.
